description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that runs on a macOS runner with a before_script that prepends /Applications/Qt/5.4/clang_64/bin to PATH, sets QTDIR to that Qt installation, changes into the src directory, writes the CI build ID into build_number.h, generates a Qt project file with qmake -project, configures the project with qmake QOwnNotes.pro -r -spec macx-clang CONFIG+=x86_64, and builds it with make. Define a build_job that is limited to the develop and master branches, inherits the before_script, and then executes the shell command `echo do some testing...`. Define a deployment_job that runs only on the master branch, inherits the same before_script, and runs a sequence of commands: execute `../gitlab-ci/create-dmg.sh`, set RELEASE_JSON_FILE to `../gitlab-ci/github-release.json`, output its contents, replace the placeholder CI_BUILD_ID with the actual $CI_BUILD_ID using sed, read the file into API_JSON, post it to the GitHub releases API with $GITHUB_ACCESS_TOKEN via curl, extract the release ID with a Python one‑liner, verify the ID is greater than zero, and finally call `../gitlab-ci/deploy-file.sh` with the release ID and token. No explicit stages, caches, or artifacts are defined; the jobs rely on the shared before_script and the default image of the runner. The pipeline uses the environment variables CI_BUILD_ID and GITHUB_ACCESS_TOKEN for versioning and authentication.
